text.title: go-http-handlers Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for go-http-handlers. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Register middleware in this order: Recovery (outermost), RequestID, Logger, Auth (on protected groups). Recovery must be outermost so panics are caught before the logger middleware runs. Logger must be after RequestID so the request ID is available in logs.
rule.rationale: Recovery placed inside Logger means a panicking handler crashes the Logger middleware before it can write the log line. The recovery response is then written by a dead middleware, producing a garbled or empty body.

rule.statement: For new services without a team-wide framework standard, prefer the stdlib 1.22 muxer (http.NewServeMux with METHOD /path patterns) over adding a framework dependency. Add a framework only when its features (validator, response helpers) justify the dependency.
rule.rationale: The 1.22 muxer supports method-specific routing (GET /users/{id}) and path parameters (r.PathValue) natively, eliminating the primary reason to reach for Gin/Echo in simple CRUD APIs.

rule.statement: Define handlers as methods on a struct that holds service dependencies (logger, services). Never use package-level globals (var DB *sql.DB) inside handlers. Inject dependencies at construction time.
rule.rationale: Package-level globals prevent parallel test execution (shared state between test cases) and make dependency substitution impossible without init() hacks.

rule.statement: Use c.ShouldBindJSON (not c.Bind) on Gin. ShouldBind returns an error without writing a response; c.Bind writes a 400 directly and the handler continues — producing a double-write bug.
rule.rationale: c.Bind's auto-response behavior is a Gin anti-pattern; handlers that check err after c.Bind may still execute, producing conflicting response writes.

rule.statement: Pass r.Context() (or c.Request.Context() in Gin) as the first argument into every service call. Never use context.Background() inside a handler.
rule.rationale: context.Background() ignores client disconnection. Long-running service calls (DB, HTTP) continue consuming resources after the client has gone away.

rule.statement: Lock the router choice (Gin, Echo, Chi, stdlib) in the system prompt for any LLM session. Reject any file where Gin and Echo imports coexist. Add a CI lint check for the unwanted import.
rule.rationale: LLMs mix handler signatures when context windows overflow. func(c *gin.Context) and func(c echo.Context) error are incompatible; mixed files produce confusing compile errors.

rule.statement: Set ReadHeaderTimeout, WriteTimeout, and IdleTimeout on http.Server. Frameworks do not set these; a server without timeouts is vulnerable to slowloris attacks and resource exhaustion.
rule.rationale: A client that never finishes sending headers holds a goroutine and file descriptor indefinitely. ReadHeaderTimeout terminates such connections.

rule.statement: Test handlers with httptest.NewRecorder + router.ServeHTTP. Cover both happy-path and unhappy-path (missing required field, invalid type, oversized body). Do not spin a real server for unit tests.
rule.rationale: httptest tests run in parallel, start instantly, and exercise the full middleware chain. Agents generating only happy-path tests miss the most common field-validation regressions.

rule.statement: Always pass r.Context() or c.Request.Context() into service calls. Never use context.Background() inside a handler.
rule.rationale: context.Background() ignores client disconnection. Long-running service calls (DB, HTTP) continue consuming resources after the client has gone away.
